data.description:
# Consolidate cross-session duplicated primitives (canonical-JSON/hash + retry/backoff)

## Context

A codebase-health audit (joe-janitor, 2026-06-24; report at `.joe-janitor/report-2026-06-24.md`)
found a **cross-session duplication** pattern — the classic fingerprint of many independent agent
sessions each solving the same sub-problem locally instead of reaching for a shared primitive. Two
clusters are material because they are **determinism-critical** (their drift silently changes
behavior), not merely stylistic:

1. **Canonical-JSON + content-hash reimplemented 6+ times, already drifted.** Sites serialize a dict
   to "sorted-key compact JSON" and most then sha256 it, but they have diverged on encoding axes
   that change the digest for equal inputs:
   - `src/rebar/_store/canonical.py:42` — `ensure_ascii=False`, the canonical home (named in
     `signing.py` docstrings) yet imported by nobody.
   - `src/rebar/_engine/rebar_reconciler/mutation.py:106` — `ensure_ascii=True` (DRIFT — verified).
   - `src/rebar/llm/workflow/schema.py:390-403` — `canonical_json` / `content_hash`.
   - `src/rebar/_engine/rebar_reconciler/conflict_resolver.py:215-219` — `_hash_value`, adds `default=str`.
   - `src/rebar/llm/plan_review/orchestrator.py:688-689` — omits `separators` (non-compact).
   - `src/rebar/signing.py:187-200` — `_canonical_payload`.
   For content-addressing / signature / cross-clone-convergence primitives, encoding drift is exactly
   the bug class that breaks determinism (two clones can compute different digests for equal inputs).

2. **7+ competing retry/backoff implementations** with hardcoded, mutually-inconsistent attempt
   counts (3/3/5/5/6/8) and three different backoff formulas:
   - `src/rebar/_store/push.py:25,122` (`_MAX_RETRIES=3`, hand-rolled loop).
   - `src/rebar/_engine/rebar_reconciler/acli_subprocess.py:234-287` (`_call_with_backoff`, `max_retries=5`, `_RETRYABLE_HTTP_CODES`).
   - `src/rebar/_engine/rebar_reconciler/acli_rest.py:29-52` (`_rest_urlopen_with_retry`, `range(3)`).
   - `src/rebar/_engine/rebar_reconciler/_advisory_lock.py:106-126,432-469` (two budgets + two backoff fns in ONE module).
   - `src/rebar/_engine/jira-capability-probe.py:46-127` (`_JQL_RETRY_COUNT=6`, fixed sleep).
   - `src/rebar/llm/runner.py:298-317` + `src/rebar/llm/structured.py:19` (single bounded LLM-validation retry).

This epic consolidates each cluster onto ONE seam, **preserving any deliberately load-bearing
variant as an explicit named parameter** rather than a silent copy. It is refactor-only: no behavior
change is intended except removing the latent determinism-drift bug in cluster (1).

## Why now / impact

- **Reliability (cluster 1, the priority):** canonical-JSON drift is a live correctness hazard for an
  event-sourced, cross-clone-convergent, HMAC-signed system. `mutation.py`'s `ensure_ascii=True`
  already differs from the canonical form; any non-ASCII ticket content hashed through divergent
  encoders can produce mismatched digests across clones/signatures.
- **Maintainability (cluster 2):** transient-failure behavior is currently unpredictable and
  untunable from one place; a single policy-parameterized helper makes it observable and adjustable.

## Scope

In scope:
- A single canonical-JSON + content-hash module (promote/extend `_store/canonical.py`), migrate the
  6 sites, audit each divergence for intent.
- A single `retry(...)` helper (predicate + backoff policy parameterized), migrate the HTTP/subprocess
  sites first, then unify the two `_advisory_lock` backoff functions.
- Characterization tests that PROVE digest-equality across the migrated sites and lock current retry
  behavior before/after.

Out of scope:
- Adopting a third-party retry lib (`tenacity`) — evaluate as a child decision, default to a small
  in-repo helper to keep core lean (`dependencies` is deliberately tiny).
- The other audit findings (#1 jsonschema dep, #3 file-size splits) — tracked separately.
- Any change to the wire format of existing signatures/digests UNLESS the audit proves a site is
  already wrong (then it is a fix, gated behind a migration note).

## Plan (proposed child tickets)

Phase A — Canonical-JSON/hash (do first; it carries the correctness risk):
- A1 (task): Characterize current behavior. Add tests capturing what each of the 6 sites currently
  emits for a fixed set of inputs incl. non-ASCII, nested, and unicode-key payloads — codifying the
  EXISTING drift so the consolidation is provably behavior-preserving (or an intentional fix).
- A2 (task): Define the canonical seam. Extend `_store/canonical.py` with `canonical_json(doc, *,
  ascii_only=False)` + `content_hash(doc)`; document the one true encoding (`ensure_ascii=False`,
  `sort_keys=True`, `separators=(",",":")`). Decide per-site whether `ascii_only` / `default=str` are
  load-bearing (cite the consumer) and expose them as explicit params, not copies.
- A3 (task): Migrate the 6 sites to import the seam; delete the local reimplementations. For
  `mutation.py`, determine whether `ensure_ascii=True` is required by any downstream consumer (acli
  manifest?) — if not, it is a drift FIX (add a migration note); if yes, call with `ascii_only=True`.
- A4 (task): Cross-clone digest-equality test — assert all migrated callers produce identical digests
  for equal inputs, and that signing/verification round-trips are unchanged.

Phase B — Retry/backoff:
- B1 (decision/task): Pick the helper shape. Default: a stdlib-only `retry(fn, *, attempts, backoff,
  retry_on, jitter)` in a new leaf module (e.g. `src/rebar/_retry.py`). Reject `tenacity` unless a
  compelling reason (keeps core deps tiny). Record the rationale.
- B2 (task): Migrate the 3 HTTP/subprocess sites (`acli_subprocess.py`, `acli_rest.py`,
  `jira-capability-probe.py`) — the clearest consolidation cluster (shared retryable-HTTP-code
  predicate). Preserve each site's current attempt count via the `attempts` arg.
- B3 (task): Unify the two `_advisory_lock` backoff functions (`_compute_backoff_seconds` +
  `_cas_backoff_seconds`) into one; keep the two distinct retry BUDGETS as config.
- B4 (task): Fold `push.py` and the LLM-validation retry (`runner.py`/`structured.py`) onto the helper
  IF they fit the predicate+backoff shape; otherwise document why they stay bespoke (different domain).

## Acceptance Criteria

- [ ] A single canonical-JSON + content-hash module is the only place that encodes "sorted-key
      compact JSON (+ sha256)"; the 6 enumerated sites import it and their local copies are deleted.
- [ ] The one true encoding is documented, and every retained variant (`ascii_only`, `default=str`,
      etc.) is an explicit, justified parameter with a cited consumer — no silent divergence remains.
- [ ] A characterization + cross-clone test proves all migrated canonical-JSON callers produce
      identical digests for equal inputs (incl. non-ASCII), and signing/verification round-trips are
      unchanged (or, where a drift was a genuine bug, a migration note documents the corrected digest).
- [ ] A single `retry(...)` helper exists; the 3 HTTP/subprocess retry sites and the two
      `_advisory_lock` backoff functions are consolidated onto it, each preserving its prior attempt
      budget; remaining bespoke retries (push/LLM) are either migrated or documented as intentionally
      separate.
- [ ] `make lint` + the full test suite pass; no new module exceeds the 800-LOC soft cap; the new
      leaf modules are ≥100 LOC or merged into an existing home (module-size policy).
- [ ] Child tickets are created and parented to this epic; each carries its own AC and file-impact.

## Success Criteria

- Determinism risk eliminated: it is impossible for two rebar clones to compute different content
  hashes for equal inputs through different code paths (enforced by the cross-clone test).
- Retry behavior is tunable from one place and observable; attempt counts are no longer scattered
  magic numbers.
- Net LOC decreases (duplication removed), and a future audit re-run finds no canonical-JSON/retry
  duplication cluster.
data.title: Consolidate cross-session duplicated primitives (canonical-JSON/hash + retry/backoff)
